title: galium palustre subsp. debile
intro: Galium palustre subsp. debile, also known as weak marsh bedstraw or weak marsh bedstraw, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the madder family (Rubiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Galium palustre subsp. debile include reducing inflammation, soothing wounds, and relieving pain. Galium palustre subsp. debile is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil for topical use.
